patterns_recognized: D-grade dual-signal-against (SP-edge-against + PQI-against) = reliably wrong direction: 8-of-10 historical instances through 5/21 confirm this as an 'expected wrong direction' marker, not merely neutral SKIP.
HARD CAP 1 (negative edge) remains the dominant EV filter: market captures 10–16pp LAD/ATL-style advantages the model systematically misses; do not bypass for any level of F-signal conviction.
F3 alone without F1/F2 backup + SP edge running against = insufficient for elevated CONFIRM: validated 5/21 WSH@NYM loss (F3-only C-grade GOLD CONFIRM lost 2-1 as Peterson outdueled Cavalli).

HARD CAP 7 (bullpen disadvantage on elevated pick) validated live: 5/20 HOU@MIN first firing was direction-correct; pattern now has live + backtest support; enforce at hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap >= 0 threshold.

Large f5_full_delta (>0.20) with confidence_downgrade=True signals a bullpen-carry dependency; if pick-side bullpen has fatigue warning, full-game conviction should not exceed B-grade regardless of F5 read.

CHC @ PIT.reasoning: Both primary signals oppose the PIT pick: SP edge 2.03 xERA against (Brown's 25.1% K% vs Mlodzinski's 20.4% clearly favors CHC), and PQI Δ=-28.1 against PIT's late-game profile. The D-grade dual-signal-against pattern has been directionally wrong in ~80% of historical instances (5/8–5/21 archive). No F-signals fire for PIT. SKIP is correct; no qualitative context overrides this grade.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against expected-wrong-direction pattern
SKIP tier correctly prevents any stake

TB @ BAL.reasoning: BAL is picked despite McClanahan (TB away SP, 25.1% K%) holding meaningful xERA advantage over Bradish (24.2% K%), and PQI running against BAL's late-game profile (Δ=-8.0). D-grade dual-signal-against pattern applies. SP and PQI both oppose the model direction — historically reliable 'expected wrong direction' marker. SKIP is correct; no new qualitative context overrides.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against expected-wrong-direction pattern
McClanahan SP quality vs Bradish confirmed by K% comparison

MIN @ CHW.reasoning: DOWNGRADE: CHW's grade C pick rests solely on F3_swing_take_gap=3311.3 while PQI (Δ=-15.5) opposes the direction and Matthews (MIN, 22.6% K%) outperforms Kay (CHW, 16.1% K%) on K-rate. Pick_prob=0.536 in the bottom bucket where non-CONFIRM picks run 31.2% historically. The 5/21 lesson (F3-alone C-grade GOLD CONFIRM without SP support lost) applies directly here. Stake already 0; DOWNGRADE signals directional caution.
lessons_applied: F3-alone-without-SP-backup insufficient for elevated CONFIRM (5/21 WSH lesson)
Bottom-bucket HARD CAP 10 caution (31.2% non-CONFIRM hit rate)
SP K% disadvantage for CHW pick side

STL @ MIL.reasoning: Misiorowski's elite 37.3% K% drives a legitimate 5.04 F1_xera_gap against Liberatore (19.75% K%); PQI strongly confirms MIL (Δ=+46.3) and Stage 1/2 agree. HARD CAP 1 correctly fires (edge=-1.87pp; market at 68.8% vs model's 66.9% — market is MORE confident in MIL). Negative edge means staking at model's inferior odds is -EV even on a direction win. No qualitative override warranted; confidence_downgrade=True flag is secondary to the strong structural case.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 EV discipline: negative-edge prevents stake even on strong directional signal
Misiorowski elite K% (37.3%) as primary SP signal
Market superiority at -1.87pp correctly enforced

NYY @ KC.reasoning: Grade A (score=5) with F1*+F2 both qualifying (HARD CAP 5 met), SP edge 0.72 xERA, PQI confirming (Δ=-18.6), and NYY's HL bullpen better (gap=-0.0175 clears HARD CAP 7). No hard caps fired. f5_full_delta=0.1792 reflects NYY's late-innings bullpen advantage, which the negative gap independently corroborates. Bullpen_fatigue flag is a caution but hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap offsets it. Stake=0 from pipeline constraint, but direction is structurally sound.
lessons_applied: F1*+F2 dual-signal qualifies: HARD CAP 5 met
hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0175 clears HARD CAP 7 check
Grade A no-cap-fired = maximum directional confidence

CIN @ NYM.reasoning: CIN picked despite Lodolo (NYM) holding 4.50 xERA advantage over McLean (small-sample, min_pa=67) and an extreme PQI headwind (Δ=+49.2 — largest PQI against on the slate). Fair_prob=0.384 (well below 0.42 floor) confirms market strongly disagrees with CIN direction. Confidence_downgrade=True and bullpen_fatigue compound the concerns. D-grade dual-signal-against is expected wrong direction; SKIP is correct.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against: SP 4.50 xERA against + PQI Δ=+49.2 both opposing CIN
Small-sample SP asterisk (McLean, min_pa=67) adds uncertainty
fair_prob 0.384 < 0.42 floor: market rejects CIN direction

ARI @ SF.reasoning: Worst grade on the slate (D, -4) with every primary signal against ARI: Roupp (SF, 26.7% K%) holds 5.35 xERA advantage over Kelly (ARI, 15.2%), PQI Δ=+42.7 strongly against ARI, and Stage 1/2 disagree massively (SF wins F5 at 74.9% yet model pivots to ARI full-game via bullpen-carry thesis with no hl_bullpen support). Confidence_downgrade=True and bullpen_fatigue add further caution. SKIP is unambiguously correct.
lessons_applied: D-grade -4: all three primary signals (SP, PQI, Stage 1/2) simultaneously oppose ARI
Bullpen-carry thesis not credible at 5.35 xERA SP disadvantage
Worst-grade pick in history: no override consideration warranted

WSH @ CLE.reasoning: Pre-cap conviction is very strong for CLE: Bibee (20.1% K%) vs Littell (12.1%) drives 3.57 xERA gap, PQI confirms strongly (Δ=+40.1), and both F1 and F5_bullpen fire. HARD CAP 7 correctly fires (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.014 means CLE's high-leverage relief is worse than WSH's in late innings). Edge=3.10pp is also below the 4pp floor. CLE's directional case (Bibee dominance) is likely correct; the caps appropriately prevent elevated staking.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 bullpen-disadvantage demotion validated (5/20-5/21 live firings)
Edge 3.10pp below 4pp floor independently prevents stake
Pre-cap A correctly demoted to B- by bullpen gap signal

PHI @ SD.reasoning: PHI has real SP edge (Luzardo 2.23 xERA better, wins F5 at 77.7%) and PQI confirmation (Δ=-35.0), but the massive f5_full_delta=0.2661 reflects severe bullpen-carry dependency as PHI's full-game edge collapses to 51.1%. Market prices SD as slight favorite (fair_prob ~54.25%), captured by HARD CAP 1 (-3.18pp) and HARD CAP 10 (bottom-bucket). Multiple caps firing correctly. Direction is marginal at best; CONFIRM the no-stake outcome.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 + HARD CAP 10 multi-cap enforcement
f5_full_delta=0.2661 large-delta bullpen-carry dependency with confidence_downgrade=True
Market reversal: fair_prob favors SD despite PHI SP advantage

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: HOU picked as marginal 50.16% full-game favorite despite TEX winning F5 at 69% (f5_full_delta=0.1918) and Rocker (TEX, 23.2% K%) holding 1.30 xERA edge over Imai (HOU, 19.2% K%, small-sample min_pa=83). PQI (Δ=+24.4) also runs against HOU. Both primary signals oppose the model direction. The bullpen-carry thesis for HOU lacks supporting evidence; HOU's 50.16% is a near-coin-flip with no structural backing. SKIP is correct.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against: SP + PQI both opposing HOU
Large f5_full_delta=0.1918 signals TEX SP dominance not offset by HOU bullpen thesis
Small-sample SP asterisk for Rocker (min_pa=83) adds uncertainty on TEX side too

MIA @ TOR.reasoning: DOWNGRADE: MIA picked despite TOR's Junk (25.8% K%) holding 1.98 xERA edge over Yesavage (16.5%) — SP AGAINST the pick — and PQI strongly opposing MIA (Δ=+30.2). Fair_prob=0.373 (well below 0.42 floor) confirms the market sees TOR as clear favorite. D-grade dual-signal-against pattern = expected wrong direction. F3=137.9 swing-take signal is marginal and insufficient without SP/PQI backup. Stake already blocked by fair_prob.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against: SP 1.98 xERA + PQI Δ=+30.2 both opposing MIA
fair_prob 0.373 < 0.42 floor: market strongly rejects MIA direction
F3-alone insufficient when SP and PQI both oppose (5/21 lesson)

COL @ LAD.reasoning: Strong structural case for LAD: F2+F3 (F3=11123.2, the largest on the slate), SP edge 0.96 xERA, PQI confirms (Δ=+17.9). HARD CAP 1 correctly fires (edge=-10.26pp; market at fair_prob=0.771 vs model's 66.83% — market 10pp more confident in LAD). This mirrors ATL@MIA and LAD@SD on 5/20 where the market correctly identified team dominance at a probability the model underestimated. Direction (LAD wins) is very likely correct; cap enforces EV discipline.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 EV discipline: 10pp market-model gap correctly blocks stake
ATL@MIA / LAD@SD 5/20 pattern: market captures home-dominant-team advantage model misses
F3=11123.2 (slate's largest) confirms LAD lineup quality despite no stake

SEA @ OAK.reasoning: HARD CAP 5 correctly caps at B (F1*=0.81 thin-sample, no F2/PQI backup) despite pre-cap A-. Luis Castillo (SEA) is a veteran quality SP — the thin-sample asterisk on his xERA comparison is appropriate caution. Edge=17.27pp above the [4,15]pp ceiling independently prevents staking. home_lineup_concentration=1.386 (OAK mildly top-heavy) vs SEA's balanced 1.041 provides slight structural note for SEA. CONFIRM the B/no-stake; no qualitative basis to elevate past the cap.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 5 F1* small-sample enforcement: F1* alone without F2/PQI cannot sustain GOLD
Edge above 15pp ceiling: calibration-suspect region, no stake
Castillo veteran SP: thin-sample asterisk caution appropriate
